meta.title: BBTI · Basketball Big Personality Test
meta.description: A scientifically baseless personality test that reads hoops fans better than you shoot free throws. 25 brutal questions, 27 archetypes, and memes in every row.

landing.subtitle: A fresh call-out every time you land here. Pass the grilling first, then we'll talk about whether you actually watch ball.
landing.errorBody: Make sure you're serving this via a local static server, not double-clicking the file.

hooks.lead: Your team loses and you blame the refs —
hooks.punch: do you actually know ball, or are you just mad?

hooks.lead: You keep talking ring culture —
hooks.punch: how many has your squad actually stacked lately?

hooks.lead: You call everyone a bandwagon rat —
hooks.punch: so whose jersey is sitting in your cart right now?

hooks.lead: Your guy teams up with a rival and you lose it —
hooks.punch: then why did your avatar change before the parade ended?

hooks.lead: "The future is his" rolls off your tongue —
hooks.punch: what year does your team's "future" actually start?

hooks.lead: You yell "that's all?" at the screen —
hooks.punch: would you even warm up if someone actually called your number?

hooks.lead: Hear "35 seconds, 13 points" and your eyes water —
hooks.punch: are you watching ball or curating a YouTube memory lane?

hooks.lead: You roast your GM in a sub thread every L —
hooks.punch: you think you could actually run a cap sheet?

hooks.lead: You swear you're not a ring chaser —
hooks.punch: then why do you own every recent champ's City Edition?

hooks.lead: Someone dares compliment a star —
hooks.punch: and you immediately drag out your top-ten list?

hooks.lead: When your team wins you rank all-time —
hooks.punch: when they lose regular-season suddenly doesn't matter?

hooks.lead: Your opener is always "Says who?" —
hooks.punch: you here to discuss or to flip the table?

hooks.lead: You mock the 73-win team for adding KD —
hooks.punch: wouldn't YOU beg your GM to push all the chips in?

hooks.lead: You screenshot the game-winner before it lands —
hooks.punch: watching ball or chasing content?

hooks.lead: Every loss: "the future is ours" —
hooks.punch: what payment plan is your team's future on?

hooks.lead: Someone mentions a play call and you scoff —
hooks.punch: then who exactly are you yelling at during timeouts?

hooks.lead: You use "58-point massacre" as a unit of measure —
hooks.punch: actually watched that game, or just repeating?

hooks.lead: A star rolls an ankle and you're emotionally broken —
hooks.punch: watching hoops or trying to be his internet mom?

hooks.lead: Airball three straight at the park, still saying "I had that" —
hooks.punch: where are you getting this confidence from?

hooks.lead: Blame the refs on a loss, brag about talent on a win —
hooks.punch: is this double-standard script saved in your Notes app?

quiz.progressCopyTemplate: Out of {total} questions, finishing this quiz already means you're built different.

result.defaultPrototype: Today you ARE your own archetype.
result.defaultTriggerNote: Matched via shortest Manhattan distance across 15 dimensions.
result.noteHiddenQ25: You hit the hidden Q25 option, and your salt gauge cleared the hidden trigger threshold.
result.noteFallbackEmpty: No comparable archetype found — dropped you on the fallback Waterboy.
result.noteFallbackDistance: The closest archetype still came in over the 18-point cutoff, so the fallback archetype kicked in.
result.roastHeading: Calling you out using your own answers
result.defaultRoastSummary: Your answers had personality, but the system hasn't organized a clean takedown yet.
result.shareError: Share action failed. Check the console for details.

gallery.title: All 27 basketball fan archetypes, in one place
gallery.body: From GOAT, IRON, and POLO to the hidden BEER and the fallback BNCH — they're all here in one complete gallery.

share.tagline: You thought you were watching the game. The game was watching you.
share.canvasInitError: Share card generation failed: canvas init error

templates: BBTI tagged me as [{code} · {name}]! {quote} Test your own basketball DNA: {url}
Verdict's in: I'm a [{name}] — {shortDesc}. BBTI read me way too well. {url}
BBTI says I'm [{code}] {name}. Accuracy is suspicious — someone's been reading my group chat. Try it: {url}

personalize.summaryTemplate: You landed on {archetypeName} not because the system glitched, but because you lean way too hard on {topLabel} and {secondLabel}, while {bottomLabel} flatlines. That's how your fandom tilted all the way into this archetype — you couldn't fake "normal" if you tried.

modelHighReads.B: You watch basketball like you're auditing the coaching staff — while everyone cheers the make, you're already checking who blew the weak-side rotation.
modelHighReads.L: Your loyalty standards are borderline religious. Switching teams, in your book, is essentially changing your last name.
modelHighReads.F: Your salt level runs hot. Watching ball isn't entertainment for you — it's a scheduled rage release.
modelHighReads.A: You don't just type fire emojis in the group chat. You actually spend the time, the money, and occasionally the dignity.
modelHighReads.S: You need an audience when you watch. If you didn't post twice, did you even watch?

modelLowReads.B: You don't sweat the details. You care more about outcomes and vibes than turning yourself into a film-breakdown machine.
modelLowReads.L: You're not a loyalty extremist, which means you've held onto the human-level sanity of "it's just a game."
modelLowReads.F: Your heat doesn't spike that fast. You can pull the brake mid-argument — borderline extinct behavior in fan circles.
modelLowReads.A: Love is love, but the second basketball asks for your wallet, weekend, or knees — your body veto kicks in first.
modelLowReads.S: You don't feel the urge to perform. You're more the stealth-viewer type, happy to watch without broadcasting.

Q2.A: You shrugged off KD's Warriors move and rolled over — your team loyalty has the depth of a hotel slipper.
Q2.B: You claim to "get it," but there's still a splinter in there. Rational brain in the front row, emotions heckling from the back.
Q2.C: KD's Hamptons flight launched you out of bed — your defection radar is set to anti-aircraft sensitivity.

Q1.B: A boss casually says "pick-and-roll is just a screen, right?" and you're immediately giving a bilingual course — your tolerance for civilians is low, but your social instincts survive.
Q1.C: You drew a set play on a napkin at a team dinner — you weren't there to eat, you were there to interview for a film-analyst job.

Q3.B: You sent smiley emojis and an ellipsis after a blown call — everyone who knows you can tell you're already past livid.
Q3.C: You didn't lose to the refs. You launched a grassroots investigation committee and dragged the league into imaginary court overnight.

Q4.B: "Consensus" instantly triggers "Says who?" in your head — you're a conditioned reflex arguer on any history topic.
Q4.C: You hijacked a table dinner into a full GOAT history lecture — you don't love discussion, you love taking over the room.

Q5.B: You made it to the arena AND worked the "proof of attendance" story — going to games and performing online are permanently coupled for you.
Q5.C: You ask about row numbers, tunnel access, and the nearest craft brewery faster than the ticketing agent — you don't attend games, you run logistics.

Q6.B: Steph hits a dagger and your first move is screenshot-and-post — you process peak moments by saving evidence before you even react.
Q6.C: You're not watching the game winner, you're turning it into a full content rollout — even your dinner has to make room for your posting cadence.

Q7.B: When your team tanks, you dig into scouting reports voluntarily — you haven't given up, you've turned despair into a to-do list.
Q7.C: 2-17 and you're more hyped every loss — you've conditioned yourself to feel pleasure through pain.

Q8.A: "He's just him / he's trash" — that answer has energy, and absolutely zero evidence.
Q8.C: The moment you pull out advanced stats, the group chat knows: this isn't a basketball convo, it's a defense of your thesis.

Q9.B: When you title a post "open discussion," you probably actually plan to act civilized for the first five minutes.
Q9.C: Mods see your username and know court's back in session — you're basically a full-time plaintiff in fan culture.

Q10.B: Your self-assessment of your hoops skill is actually close to reality — an endangered virtue at pickup courts.
Q10.C: You claimed "neighborhood Kobe" before even touching the court — your confidence is way more consistent than your shot.

Q11.B: You can hold "I still love him" and "I will never defend his new team" simultaneously — elite-tier fan multiple personality disorder.
Q11.C: Morning: trashing bandwagoners. Afternoon: avatar/wallpaper/cart all switched. Your mouth and hands are never on the same team.

Q12.B: You can resist jumping into the Kobe-vs-LeBron bait — you're not neutral, you're just tired of time-travel wars before noon.
Q12.C: A 47-page GOAT analysis doc is not fandom anymore. That's a whole side quest.

Q13.B: You got your friend to at least recognize a few star names — half a rookie mentor.
Q13.C: You got a casual to say "that's a foul," "that's not basketball," and "the future is his" — that's not evangelism, that's contamination.

Q14.B: You can name the McGrady moment on cue — you've at least passed the intro-level NBA memes certification.
Q14.C: You can recite the exact possession order — that replay isn't a memory in your head, it's a permanent desktop widget.

Q15.B: You take shots but leave an exit lane — your self-preservation instinct is at least still online.
Q15.C: You've filed your opposition research GIFs into labeled folders — this isn't argument, it's digitized revenge.

Q16.B: Your spending looks like that of a reasonable fan — wallet and passion are currently in a sustainable relationship.
Q16.C: That's not merchandise shopping. That's you wiring sponsorship fees to the league with your debit card.

Q17.B: You'll roast the front office but never defect — that stubborn-hearted mix is basically an old married couple.
Q17.C: The team is in shambles and you still cite "love" — your tolerance for misery drama is abnormal.

Q18.B: Others watch the dagger. You watch the late-game possession logic — your joy centers were re-wired by coaches.
Q18.C: Your focus on weak-side rotations and help-defense communication is on par with an unpaid assistant coach.

Q19.B: You feel the sting but don't reopen old wounds — you've been hurt, but at least it's scabbed over.
Q19.C: You turned J.R.'s run-out-the-clock moment into an annual remembrance post — trauma has fully turned into content in your hands.

Q20.B: At local watch parties, you're the reliable vibes person — not shy, not yet unhinged enough to bring your own banner.
Q20.C: Projector, banner, chants, booze — all on your phone. You don't attend the event, you ARE the event.

Q21.B: You'll grant "the numbers do look good" — you still leave a little room for the messy truth.
Q21.C: The moment someone yells "stat padder," you flip into data-defender mode and would publish a paper live.

Q22.B: Your phone has basketball bits sprinkled in, but your life isn't fully hostage to the schedule — you've got one foot halfway in.
Q22.C: Your phone reads like an unofficial team ops dashboard — basketball isn't a hobby anymore, it's running in the background 24/7.

Q23.B: You treat old memes as archaeology, not as an opportunity to recite the full lineage — you've got decent restraint.
Q23.C: Someone drops a casual meme and you launch into a full podcast retrospective — that's not a conversation, that's an oral exam.

Q24.B: People like you are the most dangerous on pickup courts — skill isn't absurd, mouth isn't idle, perfectly positioned to coach others.
Q24.C: At the park you're not a player — you're a drop-in head coach and spiritual stakeholder rolled into one.

Q25.B: Elimination games make your palms sweat — you still have the basic vitals of a normal fan.
Q25.C: Before tipoff you've already done outfit, snacks, chat-list cleanup — your ritual energy is at coronation level.
Q25.D: You've turned fandom into a booze-based project with real execution — impressive discipline, wildly wrong direction.
